Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze an event log for an insurance claims process identify cases with performance issues long durations determine the root causes based on attributes Resource Region Complexity and propose explanations and mitigation strategies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Calculate Case Durations and Step Details Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 Submit Claim End 2024 04 01 10 30 Close Claim Duration 1 5 hours Attributes Region A Low Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close No additional documents Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Ann Finance Alan CSR Jane Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 Submit Claim End 2024 04 02 11 00 Close Claim Duration 26 hours Attributes Region B Medium Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents 1 time Approve Pay Close Wait times Evaluate to Request Docs 4h 15m Request Docs to Approve 20h overnight Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Paul Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 Submit Claim End 2024 04 03 09 30 Close Claim Duration 48 hours Attributes Region A High Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents 2 times Approve Pay Close Wait times Evaluate to Request Docs 1h 20m 1st Request to 2nd Request 6h 2nd Request to Approve 23h overnight Resources CSR Jane Adjuster Mike Manager Bill Finance Alan CSR Jane Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 Submit Claim End 2024 04 01 10 45 Close Claim Duration 1 4 hours Attributes Region B Low Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Approve Pay Close No additional documents Resources CSR Mary Adjuster Lisa Manager Ann Finance Carl CSR Mary Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 Submit Claim End 2024 04 04 14 30 Close Claim Duration 77 hours Attributes Region B High Complexity Path Submit Evaluate Request Additional Documents 3 times Approve Pay Close Wait times Evaluate to Request Docs 1h 40m 1st to 2nd Request 5h 30m 2nd to 3rd Request 22h 3rd Request to Approve 19h Resources CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill Finance Carl CSR Paul

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify Cases with Performance Issues Cases 2003 and 2005 are significantly longer 48 hours and 77 hours respectively compared to the baseline of low complexity 1 5 hours and even medium complexity 26 hours Specifically Case 2005 is the worst performer

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Attributes for Root Causes Complexity Low 2001 2004 1 5 hrs 1 4 hrs Fast No document requests Medium 2002 26 hrs 1 document request High 2003 2005 48 hrs 77 hrs Multiple document requests 2 and 3 times respectively Conclusion High complexity strongly correlates with longer durations due to the need for multiple Request Additional Documents loops

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region Region A 2001 Low 1 5h 2003 High 48h Region B 2002 Med 26h 2004 Low 1 4h 2005 High 77h Conclusion Region does not seem to be the primary driver of delay within the same complexity level Low complexity in A and B are both fast High complexity in A is slow but High complexity in B is much slower

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait lets look closer at Case 2003 Region A vs Case 2005 Region B Both are high complexity 2005 took much longer and had one extra document request So complexity is the stronger driver than region but maybe Region B struggles more with high complexity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource Adjusters Adjuster Mike handled 2001 Low and 2003 High Adjuster Lisa handled 2002 Med 2004 Low and 2005 High Managers Manager Ann handled 2001 Low 2002 Med 2004 Low Manager Bill handled 2003 High 2005 High

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Conclusion High complexity cases go to Manager Bill Manager Ann handles Low and Medium Adjuster Lisa handled Case 2005 3 document requests which took the longest Is Lisa the cause or is it just the High complexity Both adjusters trigger document requests on high complexity However Adjuster Lisa requested documents three times for Case 2005 This suggests a potential resource level issue Adjuster Lisa might not be identifying all needed documents upfront leading to iterative requests

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings and Formulate Explanations Finding 1 Complexity is the primary driver of duration High complexity requires additional documents The Request Additional Documents activity is a major bottleneck especially when repeated Finding 2 Iterative Document Requests In Cases 2003 and 2005 documents were requested multiple times 2x and 3x Each request adds significant wait time likely waiting on the customer to respond Finding 3 Resource specific behavior Adjuster Lisa Case 2005 requested documents three separate times causing the longest duration in the log This indicates a potential skill or process gap she might not be gathering all requirements at once Manager Bill approves the high complexity cases but his approval times seem reasonable once documents are received Finding 4 Region No strong standalone correlation for Region as both A and B perform well on Low complexity However combining Region B High Complexity Adjuster Lisa results in the worst performance

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Develop Mitigation Strategies For Complexity Document Requests Create a comprehensive checklist for high complexity claims so adjusters request all necessary documents in a single step rather than iteratively For Resource Adjuster Lisa Provide targeted training on high complexity claims Pair her with Adjuster Mike who only needed 2 requests for a high complexity claim for mentorship Implement a peer review step for document requests on high complexity claims before they are sent to the customer Process Level Introduce a Document Completeness Check before the approval step to avoid rework loops Offer a digital portal for customers to upload all relevant documents upfront during the Submit Claim phase especially for known high complexity claim types

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Check against the prompt Did I identify cases Yes 2003 2005 Did I analyze attributes Yes Complexity Resource Region Did I propose explanations and mitigations Yes Format clearly with headings and bullet points for readability Ensure tone is analytical and professional Make sure the time math is explicitly stated to show the work
